summary: async-pipeline-builder ships a system-intel exfiltration module
details: lib/trap-core.js combines fs, os, https, and child_process to gather host information (os.hostname(), os.platform()) and POST it to network endpoints, with multiple POST sites (L385, L411, L466, L548, L549), curl invocations (L781), and repeated child_process.require usage (L12, L748, L951, L959, L964). Payloads include hostname fields (L393, L411, L553, L600, L1023) characteristic of beaconing/exfiltration of installer-side data. The module name (`trap-core`) and the breadth of system enumeration paired with outbound HTTPS POSTs are inconsistent with the package's advertised purpose (async pipeline builder); the structure matches a system-information exfiltration / command-execution payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
